### `ELFSecurityFeatures` {#elfsecurityfeatures}

<p>Captures security hardening and protection mechanisms in ELF binaries.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>symbolTableStripped</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SymbolTableStripped indicates whether debugging symbols have been removed.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>stackCanary</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">StackCanary indicates whether stack smashing protection is enabled.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>nx</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">NoExecutable indicates whether NX (no-execute) protection is enabled for the stack.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>relRO</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">RelocationReadOnly indicates the RELRO protection level.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>pie</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PositionIndependentExecutable indicates whether the binary is compiled as PIE.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>dso</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">DynamicSharedObject indicates whether the binary is a shared library.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>safeStack</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">LlvmSafeStack represents a compiler-based security mechanism that separates the stack into a safe stack for storing return addresses and other critical data, and an unsafe stack for everything else, to mitigate stack-based memory corruption errors
see https://clang.llvm.org/docs/SafeStack.html</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>cfi</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ControlFlowIntegrity represents runtime checks to ensure a program's control flow adheres to the legal paths determined at compile time, thus protecting against various types of control-flow hijacking attacks
see https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ControlFlowIntegrity.html</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>fortify</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ClangFortifySource is a broad suite of extensions to libc aimed at catching misuses of common library functions
see https://android.googlesource.com/platform//bionic/+/d192dbecf0b2a371eb127c0871f77a9caf81c4d2/docs/clang_fortify_anatomy.md</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

    </tr>
    <tr>

      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>privacyPolicyURL</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>cpeName</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>supportEnd</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>extendedSupport</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `Location` {#location}

<p>Represents a path relative to a particular filesystem resolved to a specific file.Reference.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">RealPath is the canonical absolute form of the path accessed (all symbolic links have been followed and relative path components like '.' and '..' have been removed).</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>layerID</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">FileSystemID is an ID representing and entire filesystem. For container images, this is a layer digest. For directories or a root filesystem, this is blank.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>accessPath</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">AccessPath is the path used to retrieve file contents (which may or may not have hardlinks / symlinks in the path)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>annotations</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description"></td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `PythonPdmLockEntry` {#pythonpdmlockentry}

<p>Represents a single package entry within a pdm.lock file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>summary</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Summary provides a description of the package</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#pythonpdmfileentry">PythonPdmFileEntry</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the package files with their paths and hash digests (for the base package without extras)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>marker</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Marker is the "environment" --conditional expressions that determine whether a package should be installed based on the runtime environment</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>requiresPython</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">RequiresPython specifies the Python version requirement (e.g., ">=3.6").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>dependencies</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Dependencies are the dependency specifications for the base package (without extras)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>extras</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#pythonpdmlockextravariant">PythonPdmLockExtraVariant</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Extras contains variants for different extras combinations (PDM may have multiple entries per package)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `PythonPdmLockExtraVariant` {#pythonpdmlockextravariant}

<p>Represents a specific extras combination variant within a PDM lock file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>

    </tr>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>extras</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>

    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>dependencies</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>

    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#pythonpdmfileentry">PythonPdmFileEntry</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the package files specific to this variant (only populated if different from base)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Ecosystem Specific Types {#ecosystem-specific-types}

### `AlpmDbEntry` {#alpmdbentry}

<p>Is a struct that represents the package data stored in the pacman flat-file stores for arch linux.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>basepackage</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BasePackage is the base package name this package was built from (source package in Arch build system)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>package</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Package is the package name as found in the desc file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in the desc file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>description</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Description is a human-readable package description</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>architecture</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Architecture is the target CPU architecture as defined in Arch architecture spec (e.g. x86_64, aarch64, or "any" for arch-independent packages)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>size</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Size is the installed size in bytes</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>packager</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Packager is the name and email of the person who packaged this (RFC822 format)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>url</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">URL is the upstream project URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>validation</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Validation is the validation method used for package integrity (e.g. pgp signature, sha256 checksum)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>reason</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Reason is the installation reason tracked by pacman (0=explicitly installed by user, 1=installed as dependency)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#alpmfilerecord">AlpmFileRecord</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the files installed by this package</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>backup</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#alpmfilerecord">AlpmFileRecord</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Backup is the list of configuration files that pacman backs up before upgrades</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>provides</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Provides are virtual packages provided by this package (allows other packages to depend on capabilities rather than specific packages)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>depends</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Depends are the runtime dependencies required by this package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

#### `AlpmFileRecord` {#alpmfilerecord}

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is the file path relative to the filesystem root</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>type</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Type is the file type (e.g. regular file, directory, symlink)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>uid</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">UID is the file owner user ID as recorded by pacman</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>gid</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GID is the file owner group ID as recorded by pacman</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>time</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Time is the file modification timestamp</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>size</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Size is the file size in bytes</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>link</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Link is the symlink target path if this is a symlink</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>digest</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#digest">Digest</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Digests contains file content hashes for integrity verification</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `ApkDbEntry` {#apkdbentry}

  <thead>

      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>

    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>architecture</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>

      <td class="col-description">Architecture is the target CPU architecture</td>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>url</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">URL is the upstream project URL</td>

    <tr>

      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Description is a human-readable package description</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>size</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Size is the package archive size in bytes (.apk file size)</td>

      <td class="col-description">Dependencies are the runtime dependencies required by this package</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>provides</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Provides are virtual packages provided by this package (for capability-based dependencies)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>pullChecksum</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Checksum is the package content checksum for integrity verification</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>gitCommitOfApkPort</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GitCommit is the git commit hash of the APK port definition in Alpine's aports repository</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#apkfilerecord">ApkFileRecord</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the files installed by this package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

#### `ApkFileRecord` {#apkfilerecord}

<p>Represents a single file listing and metadata from a APK DB entry (which may have many of these file records).</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>

      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is the file path relative to the filesystem root</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>ownerUid</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OwnerUID is the file owner user ID</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>ownerGid</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OwnerGID is the file owner group ID</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>permissions</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Permissions is the file permission mode string (e.g. "0755", "0644")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>digest</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#digest">Digest</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Digest is the file content hash for integrity verification</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `BitnamiSbomEntry` {#bitnamisbomentry}

<p>Represents all captured data from Bitnami packages described in Bitnami' SPDX files.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is the package name as found in the Bitnami SPDX file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>arch</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Architecture is the target CPU architecture (amd64 or arm64 in Bitnami images)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>distro</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Distro is the distribution name this package is for (base OS like debian, ubuntu, etc.)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>revision</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Revision is the Bitnami-specific package revision number (incremented for Bitnami rebuilds of same upstream version)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in the Bitnami SPDX file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is the installation path in the filesystem where the package is located</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the file paths owned by this package (tracked via SPDX relationships)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `CConanLockEntry` {#cconanlockentry}

<p>ConanV1LockEntry represents a single "node" entry from a conan.lock V1 file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>ref</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Ref is the package reference string in format name/version@user/channel</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>package_id</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PackageID is a unique package variant identifier computed from settings/options (static hash in Conan 1.x, can have collisions with complex dependency graphs)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>prev</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Prev is the previous lock entry reference for versioning</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>requires</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Requires are the runtime package dependencies</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>build_requires</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildRequires are the build-time dependencies (e.g. cmake, compilers)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>py_requires</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PythonRequires are the Python dependencies needed for Conan recipes</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>options</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>KeyValues</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Options are package configuration options as key-value pairs (e.g. shared=True, fPIC=True)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is the filesystem path to the package in Conan cache</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>context</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Context is the build context information</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `CConanLockV2Entry` {#cconanlockv2entry}

<p>ConanV2LockEntry represents a single "node" entry from a conan.lock V2 file.</p>

      <td class="col-description">Ref is the package reference string in format name/version@user/channel</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>packageID</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PackageID is a unique package variant identifier (dynamic in Conan 2.0, more accurate than V1)</td>
    </tr>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>username</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>recipeRevision</code></td>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>packageRevision</code></td>

    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>timestamp</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">TimeStamp is when this package was built/locked</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `CocoaPodfileLockEntry` {#cocoapodfilelockentry}

<table class="schema-table">

      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>

### `CondaMetadataEntry` {#condametadataentry}

<p>CondaMetaPackage represents metadata for a Conda package extracted from the conda-meta/*.json files.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>arch</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Arch is the target CPU architecture for the package (e.g., "arm64", "x86_64").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is the package name as found in the conda-meta JSON file.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in the conda-meta JSON file.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>build</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Build is the build string identifier (e.g., "h90dfc92_1014").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>build_number</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildNumber is the sequential build number for this version.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>channel</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Channel is the Conda channel URL where the package was retrieved from.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>subdir</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Subdir is the subdirectory within the channel (e.g., "osx-arm64", "linux-64").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>noarch</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Noarch indicates if the package is platform-independent (e.g., "python", "generic").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>license</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">License is the package license identifier.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>license_family</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">LicenseFamily is the general license category (e.g., "MIT", "Apache", "GPL").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>md5</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">MD5 is the MD5 hash of the package archive.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sha256</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SHA256 is the SHA-256 hash of the package archive.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>size</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Size is the package archive size in bytes.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>timestamp</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Timestamp is the Unix timestamp when the package was built.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>fn</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Filename is the original package archive filename (e.g., "zlib-1.2.11-h90dfc92_1014.tar.bz2").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>url</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">URL is the full download URL for the package archive.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>extracted_package_dir</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ExtractedPackageDir is the local cache directory where the package was extracted.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>depends</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Depends is the list of runtime dependencies with version constraints.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files is the list of files installed by this package.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>paths_data</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#condapathsdata">CondaPathsData</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PathsData contains detailed file metadata from the paths.json file.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>link</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#condalink">CondaLink</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Link contains installation source metadata from the link.json file.</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>repository</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Repository is the source code repository URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>documentation</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Documentation is the documentation site URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>publish_to</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PublishTo is the package repository to publish to, or "none" to prevent accidental publishing</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>environment</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#dartpubspecenvironment">DartPubspecEnvironment</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Environment is SDK version constraints for Dart and Flutter</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>platforms</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Platforms are the supported platforms (Android, iOS, web, etc.)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>ignored_advisories</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">IgnoredAdvisories are the security advisories to explicitly ignore for this package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

#### `DartPubspecEnvironment` {#dartpubspecenvironment}

<p>Represents SDK version constraints from the environment section of pubspec.yaml.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sdk</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SDK is the Dart SDK version constraint (e.g. ">=2.12.0 <3.0.0")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>flutter</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Flutter is the Flutter SDK version constraint if this is a Flutter package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `DartPubspecLockEntry` {#dartpubspeclockentry}

<p>Is a struct that represents a single entry found in the "packages" section in a Dart pubspec.lock file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is the package name as found in the pubspec.lock file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in the pubspec.lock file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>hosted_url</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">HostedURL is the URL of the package repository for hosted packages (typically pub.dev, but can be custom repository identified by hosted-url). When PUB_HOSTED_URL environment variable changes, lockfile tracks the source.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>vcs_url</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">VcsURL is the URL of the VCS repository for git/path dependencies (for packages fetched from version control systems like Git)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `DotnetDepsEntry` {#dotnetdepsentry}

<p>Is a struct that represents a single entry found in the "libraries" section in a .NET [*.]deps.json file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is the package name as found in the deps.json file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in the deps.json file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is the relative path to the package within the deps structure (e.g. "app.metrics/3.0.0")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sha512</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Sha512 is the SHA-512 hash of the NuGet package content WITHOUT the signed content for verification (won't match hash from NuGet API or manual calculation of .nupkg file)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>hashPath</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">HashPath is the relative path to the .nupkg.sha512 hash file (e.g. "app.metrics.3.0.0.nupkg.sha512")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>executables</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Executables are the map of .NET Portable Executable files within this package with their version resources</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `DotnetPortableExecutableEntry` {#dotnetportableexecutableentry}

<p>Is a struct that represents a single entry found within "VersionResources" section of a .NET Portable Executable binary file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>

      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">AssemblyVersion is the .NET assembly version number (strong-named version)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>legalCopyright</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">LegalCopyright is the copyright notice string</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>comments</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Comments are additional comments or description embedded in PE resources</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>internalName</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">InternalName is the internal name of the file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>companyName</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">CompanyName is the company that produced the file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>productName</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ProductName is the name of the product this file is part of</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>productVersion</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ProductVersion is the version of the product (may differ from AssemblyVersion)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `DpkgArchiveEntry` {#dpkgarchiveentry}

<p>Represents package metadata extracted from a .deb archive file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>package</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Package is the package name as found in the status file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>source</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Source is the source package name this binary was built from (one source can produce multiple binary packages)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the binary package version as found in the status file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sourceVersion</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SourceVersion is the source package version (may differ from binary version when binNMU rebuilds occur)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>architecture</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Architecture is the target architecture per Debian spec (specific arch like amd64/arm64, wildcard like any, architecture-independent "all", or "source" for source packages)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>maintainer</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Maintainer is the package maintainer's name and email in RFC822 format (name must come first, then email in angle brackets)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>installedSize</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">InstalledSize is the total size of installed files in kilobytes</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>provides</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Provides are the virtual packages provided by this package (allows other packages to depend on capabilities. Can include versioned provides like "libdigest-md5-perl (= 2.55.01)")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>depends</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Depends are the packages required for this package to function (will not be installed unless these requirements are met, creates strict ordering constraint)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>preDepends</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PreDepends are the packages that must be installed and configured BEFORE even starting installation of this package (stronger than Depends, discouraged unless absolutely necessary as it adds strict constraints for apt)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#dpkgfilerecord">DpkgFileRecord</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the files installed by this package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `ElfBinaryPackageNoteJsonPayload` {#elfbinarypackagenotejsonpayload}

<p>Represents metadata captured from the .note.package section of an ELF-formatted binary</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>type</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Type is the type of the package (e.g. "rpm", "deb", "apk", etc.)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>architecture</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Architecture of the binary package (e.g. "amd64", "arm", etc.)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>osCPE</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OSCPE is a CPE name for the OS, typically corresponding to CPE_NAME in os-release (e.g. cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:33)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>os</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OS is the OS name, typically corresponding to ID in os-release (e.g. "fedora")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>osVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">osVersion is the version of the OS, typically corresponding to VERSION_ID in os-release (e.g. "33")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>system</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">System is a context-specific name for the system that the binary package is intended to run on or a part of</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>vendor</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Vendor is the individual or organization that produced the source code for the binary</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sourceRepo</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SourceRepo is the URL to the source repository for which the binary was built from</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>commit</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Commit is the commit hash of the source repository for which the binary was built from</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is the package name as found in the mix.lock file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in the mix.lock file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>pkgHash</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PkgHash is the outer checksum (SHA-256) of the entire Hex package tarball for integrity verification (preferred method, replaces deprecated inner checksum)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>pkgHashExt</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PkgHashExt is the extended package hash format (inner checksum is deprecated - SHA-256 of concatenated file contents excluding CHECKSUM file, now replaced by outer checksum)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

    </tr>
    <tr>

      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>

    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>pkgHash</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">PkgHash is the outer checksum (SHA-256) of the entire Hex package tarball for integrity verification (preferred method over deprecated inner checksum)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>pkgHashExt</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>

### `GoModuleBuildinfoEntry` {#gomodulebuildinfoentry}

<p>GolangBinaryBuildinfoEntry represents all captured data for a Golang binary</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>goBuildSettings</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>KeyValues</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildSettings contains the Go build settings and flags used to compile the binary (e.g., GOARCH, GOOS, CGO_ENABLED).</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>goCompiledVersion</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GoCompiledVersion is the version of Go used to compile the binary.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>architecture</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Architecture is the target CPU architecture for the binary (extracted from GOARCH build setting).</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>h1Digest</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">H1Digest is the Go module hash in h1: format for the main module from go.sum.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>mainModule</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">MainModule is the main module path for the binary (e.g., "github.com/anchore/syft").</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>goCryptoSettings</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GoCryptoSettings contains FIPS and cryptographic configuration settings if present.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>goExperiments</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GoExperiments lists experimental Go features enabled during compilation (e.g., "arenas", "cgocheck2").</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>virtualPath</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>

</table>

#### `JavaPomProject` {#javapomproject}

<p>Represents fields of interest extracted from a Java archive's pom.xml file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>

      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is path to the pom.xml file within the archive</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>parent</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#javapomparent">JavaPomParent</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Parent is the parent POM reference for inheritance (child POMs inherit configuration from parent)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>groupId</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GroupID is Maven group identifier (reversed domain name like org.apache.maven)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>artifactId</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ArtifactID is Maven artifact identifier (project name)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is project version (together with groupId and artifactId forms Maven coordinates groupId:artifactId:version)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is a human-readable project name (displayed in Maven-generated documentation)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>description</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Description is detailed project description</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>url</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">URL is the project URL (typically project website or repository)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is path to the pom.properties file within the archive</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is the project name</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>groupId</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">GroupID is Maven group identifier uniquely identifying the project across all projects (follows reversed domain name convention like com.company.project)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>artifactId</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ArtifactID is Maven artifact identifier, the name of the jar/artifact (unique within the groupId scope)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is artifact version</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>scope</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Scope is dependency scope determining when dependency is available (compile=default all phases, test=test compilation/execution only, runtime=runtime and test not compile, provided=expected from JDK or container)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>extraFields</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Extra is additional custom properties not in standard Maven coordinates</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>osArch</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OsArch is the target CPU architecture</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>osName</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OsName is the name of the target runtime operating system environment</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>osVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OsVersion is the version of the target runtime operating system environment</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>source</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Source refers to the origin repository of OpenJDK source</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>buildSource</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildSource Git SHA of the build repository</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>buildSourceRepo</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildSourceRepo refers to rhe repository URL for the build source</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sourceRepo</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SourceRepo refers to the OpenJDK repository URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>fullVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">FullVersion is extracted from the 'java.runtime.version' JVM property</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>semanticVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SemanticVersion is derived from the OpenJDK version</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>buildInfo</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildInfo contains additional build information</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>jvmVariant</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">JvmVariant specifies the JVM variant (e.g., Hotspot or OpenJ9)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>jvmVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">JvmVersion is extracted from the 'java.vm.version' JVM property</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>imageType</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ImageType can be 'JDK' or 'JRE'</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>buildType</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildType can be 'commercial' (used in some older oracle JDK distributions)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `JavascriptNpmPackage` {#javascriptnpmpackage}

    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version as found in package.json</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>author</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Author is package author name</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>homepage</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Homepage is project homepage URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>description</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Description is a human-readable package description</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>url</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">URL is repository or project URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>private</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Private is whether this is a private package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `JavascriptNpmPackageLockEntry` {#javascriptnpmpackagelockentry}

<p>NpmPackageLockEntry represents a single entry within the "packages" section of a package-lock.json file.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>resolved</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Resolved is URL where this package was downloaded from (registry source)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>integrity</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Integrity is Subresource Integrity hash for verification using standard SRI format (sha512-... or sha1-...). npm changed from SHA-1 to SHA-512 in newer versions. For registry sources this is the integrity from registry, for remote tarballs it's SHA-512 of the file. npm verifies tarball matches this hash before unpacking, throwing EINTEGRITY error if mismatch detected.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>dependencies</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Dependencies is a map of dependencies and their version markers, i.e. "lodash": "^1.0.0"</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `LinuxKernelArchive` {#linuxkernelarchive}

<p>LinuxKernel represents all captured data for a Linux kernel</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is kernel name (typically "Linux")</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>architecture</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Architecture is the target CPU architecture</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is kernel version string</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>extendedVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">ExtendedVersion is additional version information</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>buildTime</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">BuildTime is when the kernel was built</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>author</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Author is who built the kernel</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>format</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Format is kernel image format (e.g. bzImage, zImage)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>rwRootFS</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>bool</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">RWRootFS is whether root filesystem is mounted read-write</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>swapDevice</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SwapDevice is swap device number</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>rootDevice</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>int</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">RootDevice is root device number</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>videoMode</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">VideoMode is default video mode setting</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `LinuxKernelModule` {#linuxkernelmodule}

<p>Represents a loadable kernel module (.ko file) with its metadata, parameters, and dependencies.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is module name</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is module version string</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>sourceVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">SourceVersion is the source code version identifier</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is the filesystem path to the .ko kernel object file (absolute path)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>description</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Description is a human-readable module description</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>author</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Author is module author name and email</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>license</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">License is module license (e.g. GPL, BSD) which must be compatible with kernel</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>kernelVersion</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">KernelVersion is kernel version this module was built for</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>versionMagic</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">VersionMagic is version magic string for compatibility checking (includes kernel version, SMP status, module loading capabilities like "3.17.4-302.fc21.x86_64 SMP mod_unload modversions"). Module will NOT load if vermagic doesn't match running kernel.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>parameters</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Parameters are the module parameters that can be configured at load time (user-settable values like module options)</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### `NixStoreEntry` {#nixstoreentry}

<p>Represents a package in the Nix store (/nix/store) with its derivation information and metadata.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is full store path for this output (e.g. /nix/store/abc123...-package-1.0)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>output</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Output is the specific output name for multi-output packages (empty string for default "out" output, can be "bin", "dev", "doc", etc.)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>outputHash</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">OutputHash is hash prefix of the store path basename (first part before the dash)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>derivation</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#nixderivation">NixDerivation</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Derivation is information about the .drv file that describes how this package was built</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>files</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Files are the list of files under the nix/store path for this package</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

#### `NixDerivation` {#nixderivation}

<p>Represents a Nix .drv file that describes how to build a package including inputs, outputs, and build instructions.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is path to the .drv file in Nix store</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>system</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">System is target system string indicating where derivation can be built (e.g. "x86_64-linux", "aarch64-darwin"). Must match current system for local builds.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>inputDerivations</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#nixderivationreference">NixDerivationReference</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">InputDerivations are the list of other derivations that were inputs to this build (dependencies)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>inputSources</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">InputSources are the list of source file paths that were inputs to this build</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

#### `NixDerivationReference` {#nixderivationreference}

<p>Represents a reference to another derivation used as a build input or runtime dependency.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>path</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Path is path to the referenced .drv file</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>outputs</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Outputs are which outputs of the referenced derivation were used (e.g. ["out"], ["bin", "dev"])</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Homepage is project homepage URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>dependencies</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>

### `PhpComposerInstalledEntry` {#phpcomposerinstalledentry}

<p>Represents a single package entry from a composer v1/v2 "installed.json" files (very similar to composer.lock files).</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>name</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Name is package name in vendor/package format (e.g. symfony/console)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>version</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Version is the package version</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>source</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#phpcomposerexternalreference">PhpComposerExternalReference</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Source is the source repository information for development (typically git repo, used when passing --prefer-source). Originates from source code repository.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>dist</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code><a href="#phpcomposerexternalreference">PhpComposerExternalReference</a></code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Dist is distribution archive information for production (typically zip/tar, default install method). Packaged version of released code.</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>require</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Require is runtime dependencies with version constraints (package will not install unless these requirements can be met)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>provide</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Provide is virtual packages/functionality provided by this package (allows other packages to depend on capabilities)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>require-dev</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">RequireDev is development-only dependencies (not installed in production, only when developing this package or running tests)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>suggest</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>obj</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Suggest is optional but recommended dependencies (suggestions for packages that would extend functionality)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>license</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">License is the list of license identifiers (SPDX format)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>type</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Type is package type indicating purpose (library=reusable code, project=application, metapackage=aggregates dependencies, etc.)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>notification-url</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">NotificationURL is the URL to notify when package is installed (for tracking/statistics)</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>bin</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Bin is the list of binary/executable files that should be added to PATH</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>authors</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;<a href="#phpcomposerauthors">PhpComposerAuthors</a>&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Authors are the list of package authors with name/email/homepage</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>description</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Description is a human-readable package description</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>homepage</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Homepage is project homepage URL</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>keywords</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>Array&lt;str&gt;</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Keywords are the list of keywords for package discovery/search</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>time</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Time is timestamp when this package version was released</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

#### `PhpComposerExternalReference` {#phpcomposerexternalreference}

<p>Represents source or distribution information for a PHP package, indicating where the package code is retrieved from.</p>

<table class="schema-table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th class="col-field-name">Field Name</th>
      <th class="col-type">Type</th>
      <th class="col-description">Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>type</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>
      <td class="col-description">Type is reference type (git for source VCS, zip/tar for dist archives)</td>
    </tr>

      <td class="col-field-name"><code>url</code><svg class="required-icon"><use xlink:href="#icon-required"></use></svg></td>

    <tr>
      <td class="col-field-name"><code>shasum</code></td>
      <td class="col-type"><code>str</code></td>

### `PythonPackage` {#pythonpackage}
